title: Graham Platner ends Senate bid. And, why Nolan Wells' death captured national attention
content:
# 国际新闻英文阅读

## 新闻标题
Graham Platner ends Senate bid. And, why Nolan Wells' death captured national attention

## 新闻背景
这篇NPR新闻简报涵盖多个国际与国内新闻：美国民主党参选人格雷厄姆·普拉特纳因前女友指控其强奸而退出缅因州参议员竞选；美国对伊朗南部发动空袭，伊朗报复袭击美军在科威特和巴林的基地；18岁少年诺兰·威尔斯在独立日旅行中神秘死亡，家人聘请民权律师调查；以及内华达州一个曾经繁荣的赌场小镇正在被家族努力复兴。

## 英文原文
Democrat Graham Platner announced last night that he has ended his U.S. Senate campaign in Maine. The politician faced pressure from party leaders to end his bid after one of his former girlfriends accused him of rape. He denies this allegation. In a video, Platner said his decision to drop out was not an admission of guilt and instead blamed a "political system not built for normal people." Now the race is on for Democrats to find a new candidate to replace Platner before the state's July 27 deadline to name someone new.

Overnight, the U.S. launched strikes on southern Iran, targeting around 90 military sites along the coast and the vital Strait of Hormuz, according to the U.S. Central Command. In retaliation, Iran launched air attacks in Kuwait and Bahrain against U.S. military installations. Kuwait's military reported intercepting missiles and drones. Countries in the Middle East are on high alert as they brace for the possibility of further conflict in the region.

The family of 18-year-old Nolan Wells, who was found dead on Horn Island off the coast of Mississippi after a Fourth of July getaway with friends, has enlisted the help of civil rights attorney Benjamin Crump. It's still a mystery why Wells, last seen at the northwest tip of the island around 3 p.m. on July 4, was the only one from his group who didn't return. The case has garnered national attention online. Crump announced that his office will conduct an independent investigation into the circumstances of Wells' death. In a recent interview with Don Lemon, Crump said that officials have yet to inform the family about any suspicions of foul play. The Jackson County Sheriff's Office is calling for people to submit any photos or videos that could help determine what happened the day Wells went missing as they continue their investigation.

Summer can be an exciting time for adventure, but it has some risks. Extreme heat, tempting open bodies of water and outdoor critters can all prove deadly. But don't let that hold you back from having fun. Here are proactive ways to keep yourself safe this season.

A determined family is taking on a challenge like no other: resurrecting a town. Primm, Nevada, was once a bustling, family-friendly casino hotspot. But it has experienced a steep decline and has gradually become a ghost town as more casino options have emerged across the United States. In the 1990s and 2000s, Primm attracted visitors with affordable accommodations, gambling, concerts, rodeos and attractions like a roller coaster. Since then, that ride has shut down, and two of the town's three hotels have closed in recent years. The once-thriving outlet mall now features only a single thrift store. When the Primm family learned that the last operating casino-hotel, Primm Valley Resort & Casino, was on the brink of closure, they joined forces with the Nevada-based convenience store chain Terrible's to breathe new life into this fading town.

The Kennedy Center on June 28, with its facade signage still covered by a tarp and scaffolding.

This newsletter was edited by Suzanne Nuyen.

## 高频词汇学习

- **campaign** [kæmˈpeɪn]
  - 名词
  - 中文释义：竞选活动；运动
  - 原文例句：Democrat Graham Platner announced last night that he has ended his U.S. Senate campaign in Maine.
  - 新闻语境用法：指政治选举活动，常与election、Senate等词搭配。

- **allegation** [ˌæləˈɡeɪʃən]
  - 名词
  - 中文释义：指控；声称
  - 原文例句：He denies this allegation.
  - 新闻语境用法：指未经证实的指控，常用于法律和政治报道中。

- **retaliation** [rɪˌtæliˈeɪʃən]
  - 名词
  - 中文释义：报复；反击
  - 原文例句：In retaliation, Iran launched air attacks in Kuwait and Bahrain against U.S. military installations.
  - 新闻语境用法：指针对对方行动采取的报复性措施，常见于军事冲突报道。

- **intercept** [ˌɪntərˈsept]
  - 动词
  - 中文释义：拦截；截获
  - 原文例句：Kuwait's military reported intercepting missiles and drones.
  - 新闻语境用法：指阻止敌方武器或通信到达目标，常用于军事新闻。

- **garner** [ˈɡɑːrnər]
  - 动词
  - 中文释义：获得；收集
  - 原文例句：The case has garnered national attention online.
  - 新闻语境用法：指吸引或获得某种关注、支持或资源。

- **independent** [ˌɪndɪˈpendənt]
  - 形容词
  - 中文释义：独立的；自主的
  - 原文例句：Crump announced that his office will conduct an independent investigation into the circumstances of Wells' death.
  - 新闻语境用法：指不受外界干预的、自主进行的调查或判断。

- **circumstances** [ˈsɜːrkəmstænsɪz]
  - 名词（复数）
  - 中文释义：情况；环境；细节
  - 原文例句：an independent investigation into the circumstances of Wells' death.
  - 新闻语境用法：指事件发生时的具体情境或相关事实。

- **resurrect** [ˌrezəˈrekt]
  - 动词
  - 中文释义：复活；复兴；重新使用
  - 原文例句：A determined family is taking on a challenge like no other: resurrecting a town.
  - 新闻语境用法：指使衰败或消失的事物重新焕发生机。

- **steep** [stiːp]
  - 形容词
  - 中文释义：急剧的；陡峭的
  - 原文例句：But it has experienced a steep decline and has gradually become a ghost town.
  - 新闻语境用法：形容下降或上升的速度非常快、幅度大。

- **on the brink of** [ɒn ðə brɪŋk ɒv]
  - 短语
  - 中文释义：濒临；即将
  - 原文例句：the last operating casino-hotel... was on the brink of closure.
  - 新闻语境用法：表示某事物即将发生或处于危险边缘。

## 重点表达解析

- **drop out**
  - 含义：退出；退学；退出竞选
  - 使用场景：常用于描述退出比赛、选举、学校或某项活动
  - 例句：Platner said his decision to drop out was not an admission of guilt.

- **on high alert**
  - 含义：高度戒备；处于警戒状态
  - 使用场景：用于描述国家、军队或组织面临潜在威胁时的紧急状态
  - 例句：Countries in the Middle East are on high alert as they brace for the possibility of further conflict.

- **breathe new life into**
  - 含义：注入新活力；使...焕然一新
  - 使用场景：用于描述改造、复兴或振兴某事物
  - 例句：They joined forces to breathe new life into this fading town.

- **enlist the help of**
  - 含义：寻求...的帮助；争取...的支持
  - 使用场景：正式用语，常用于寻求专业人士或组织的协助
  - 例句：The family has enlisted the help of civil rights attorney Benjamin Crump.

- **prove deadly**
  - 含义：证明是致命的；可能导致死亡
  - 使用场景：用于描述潜在危险或风险
  - 例句：Extreme heat, tempting open bodies of water and outdoor critters can all prove deadly.

## 阅读理解练习

1. 为什么格雷厄姆·普拉特纳退出参议员竞选？
   A. 他承认了强奸指控
   B. 他指责政治体系不适合普通人
   C. 他因健康原因退出
   D. 他失去了党内支持

   答案：B

2. 美国对伊朗发动空袭后，伊朗采取了什么行动？
   A. 立即投降
   B. 对科威特和巴林的美国军事设施发动空袭
   C. 向联合国申诉
   D. 关闭霍尔木兹海峡

   答案：B

3. 关于诺兰·威尔斯的案件，以下哪项描述是正确的？
   A. 警方已经确认他是被谋杀的
   B. 他的家人聘请了民权律师进行独立调查
   C. 他是和朋友们一起返回的
   D. 案件没有引起公众关注

   答案：B
